note: My task only requires adding a with_counts parameter to the groupby filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (function do_groupby). If your feature also touches filters.py, let's split by function/section so we don't clobber each other - tell me which function(s) you need to edit there.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby() filter (do_groupby/do_async_groupby) in src/jinja2/filters.py. This is the only file I need to touch. If your feature also needs filters.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping functions/regions within it so we can merge cleanly.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Our features (with_counts, case_sensitive) both touch the same functions (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby), so a disjoint split isn't literally possible. Proposal: sequence our edits instead. I'll go first - implement with_counts now, run tests, then commit my change (git commit) so it's captured as a checkpoint. I'll send DONE when committed. You then implement case_sensitive on top of that commit and your own git diff (uncommitted working tree changes) will cleanly show only your delta for your patch.txt. If you'd rather go first instead, say so now and I'll wait. Otherwise I'll proceed immediately.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Our features both touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in the same file/functions, so a disjoint file split isn't possible - we need to sequence edits instead. Plan: I'll edit the clean file now (add case_sensitive), immediately save my diff to patch.txt, then revert the working tree back to the clean original state (git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py) so you get a pristine file to add with_counts on top of. I'll send DONE once I've reverted and it's safe for you to start editing. Please wait for that DONE before touching filters.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed - your plan works well and avoids extra commits. I'll wait for your DONE (confirming the file is reverted to clean state) before touching filters.py, then I'll implement with_counts, save my own patch.txt, and send DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Implemented case_sensitive param on sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, ran full test suite (837 passed), saved my diff to patch.txt, then reverted src/jinja2/filters.py to the clean original state (verified via git diff --stat = empty). The file is now pristine - safe for you to implement with_counts on it. My patch.txt is self-contained and won't be touched again.
